logs.title: MBR (1587 lines) and EBR (1080 lines) ingested — AML-2024-0271 loaded
reasoning: MBR reference: MPR-AML-5-v06 (effective 01-Jan-2024) — 1587 lines parsed across 17 sections + appendices
EBR batch: AML-2024-0271 — 1080 lines parsed covering dispensing through packaging and QC
Product: Amlodipine Besylate Tablets 5mg, Batch Size 30,000 tablets, Mfg Date 19-Jul-2024
Manufacturing date range: 19-Jul-2024 (dispensing through coating) to 20-Jul-2024 (packaging)
Both documents structured and ready for 10-category, 68-point GMP review

logs.title: 68-point checklist: 62 pass, 6 fail — OOS assay suppressed + unauthorized retesting
reasoning: Category 1–3 (Header, BOM, Equipment): All PASS — product identity, batch number, MBR version, materials, calibrations all verified
Category 4–6 (Process, IPC, Environment): All PASS — CPPs in range, IPC results clean, EM within limits throughout
Category 7–8 (Yield/Reconciliation, QC): Yield PASS; QC — 5 tests pass, 1 critical failure in HPLC assay data integrity
Category 9–10 (Signatures, Deviations): Signatures mostly complete (QA Manager PENDING); CRITICAL — 6 checklist items incorrectly marked Y
Total: 62 pass / 6 fail (Items 60, 62, 63, 64, BR-12 trigger missed, BR-14 trigger missed)

logs.title: 4 findings confirmed: 2 CRITICAL + 2 MAJOR — Confidence: 52% — HOLD
reasoning: CRITICAL-1: OOS assay result (91.2% vs spec 95.0–105.0%) obtained on first analysis — not reported to QA, no OOS notification (Form OOS-001) filed, batch not placed on HOLD (MBR Section 6.3.1 violated)

CRITICAL-2: Original OOS result recorded only on loose, uncontrolled worksheet stapled to EBR — not entered in main batch record; only passing retest (97.8%) appears in Section 9.2.2 (MBR Section 12.1 Rule 6 violated)
MAJOR-1: Unauthorized retesting performed — retest done without QA notification, without approved Retest Protocol (Form OOS-RP-001), and without Phase I investigation (MBR Section 6.3.2 violated)
MAJOR-2: Section 9.2.3 First Analysis Verification field shows 'N' with blank retest protocol number — QA reviewer Sneha Patil signed Items 63 and 64 as Y without actioning this red flag
Confidence: 100 − 15 (critical-1) − 15 (critical-2) − 8 (major-1) − 8 (major-2) − 2 (obs: QA Manager sig PENDING) = 52% — mandatory full human review

logs.title: QA escalation drafted — awaiting send approval
reasoning: First HPLC assay result 91.2% (OOS vs spec 95.0–105.0%) suppressed — not entered in official batch record
Unauthorized retest (97.8%) recorded in Section 9.2.2 without OOS protocol, QA notification, or Phase I investigation
Unauthorized average (91.2 + 97.8) / 2 = 94.5% noted on uncontrolled worksheet — still OOS under spec
QA reviewer Sneha Patil (QA-4010) and analyst Pooja Iyer (QC-3022) both require formal review
Confidence 52% — mandatory full human review; formal Data Integrity Investigation and retroactive OOS Phase I required

artifacts.label: QA Escalation — OOS Suppression + Data Integrity
artifacts.subject: HOLD: Data Integrity Violation — Batch AML-2024-0271 Amlodipine 5mg
artifacts.body:
Dear QA Head,

Batch AML-2024-0271 (Amlodipine Besylate Tablets 5mg) has been placed on HOLD.

CRITICAL FINDING 1 — OOS Result Suppressed:
First HPLC assay result: 91.2% (spec 95.0–105.0%) — OOS. Not reported to QA. No Form OOS-001 filed. Batch not placed on HOLD. MBR Section 6.3.1 violated.

CRITICAL FINDING 2 — Original Data Excluded:
OOS result recorded only on loose uncontrolled worksheet stapled to EBR. Only passing retest (97.8%) entered in Section 9.2.2. MBR Section 12.1 Rule 6 violated.

MAJOR FINDING 1 — Unauthorized Retesting:
Retest performed without QA notification, approved Retest Protocol (Form OOS-RP-001), or Phase I investigation. MBR Section 6.3.2 violated.

MAJOR FINDING 2 — QA Reviewer Failure:
Section 9.2.3 shows First Analysis Verification = N with blank retest protocol number. QA reviewer Sneha Patil (QA-4010) signed Items 63 and 64 as Y without flagging this.

Required actions:
1. Open formal Data Integrity Investigation (SOP-DI-001)
2. Open retroactive OOS Phase I Investigation for 91.2% result
3. Review analyst Pooja Iyer (QC-3022) and QA reviewer Sneha Patil (QA-4010)

Batch remains on HOLD.

Regards,
Pace — Batch Record Review
Lilly Quality Operations
